data.description:

Anteris Technologies ( (AU:AVR) ) has issued an update.

Anteris Technologies Global Corp. has notified the ASX of a proposed placement involving the issue of up to 55,652,173 shares of common stock, with a targeted issue date of 23 January 2026. The capital raising via this substantial new share issuance is expected to bolster the company’s financial position and support its ongoing operational and strategic initiatives, with implications for shareholder dilution but also potentially enhancing its capacity to advance product development and market expansion within the medtech sector.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:AVR) stock is a Buy with a A$10.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Anteris Technologies stock, see the AU:AVR Stock Forecast page.

More about Anteris Technologies

Anteris Technologies Global Corp., listed on the ASX under the code AVR, operates in the medical technology sector, focusing on innovative cardiovascular solutions and related healthcare technologies for global markets.

The latest announcement is out from Morphic Ethical Equities Fund Ltd. ( (AU:MEC) ).

Morphic Ethical Equities Fund Limited has released its estimated unaudited net tangible assets per share as at 9 January 2026, reporting a pre‑tax NTA of $1.3989 and a post‑tax NTA of $1.3304, compared with a closing share price of $1.18. The figures indicate the shares are trading at a discount to the company’s underlying asset backing, a dynamic that may be closely watched by existing and prospective investors assessing valuation and market sentiment toward the ethical investment vehicle.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:MEC) stock is a Buy with a A$1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Morphic Ethical Equities Fund Ltd. stock, see the AU:MEC Stock Forecast page.

More about Morphic Ethical Equities Fund Ltd.

Morphic Ethical Equities Fund Limited is an Australian listed investment company focused on ethical and responsible global equity investments, offering investors exposure to a diversified portfolio screened on environmental, social and governance (ESG) criteria. The fund trades on the Australian Securities Exchange under the ticker MEC and targets investors seeking ethically managed equity exposure with transparent reporting of portfolio value.

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

MA Credit Income Trust ( (AU:MA1) ) has provided an announcement.

The December quarter 2025 investor update for MA Credit Income Trust outlines the governance and fee structure of the fund, emphasising that Equity Trustees serves as Responsible Entity while MA Financial group entities manage the portfolio and receive management fees of 0.90% per annum on direct credit investments plus other fees from underlying vehicles. The release stresses that the material is general information only, past performance is not indicative of future returns, and that investments in the fund carry risks including potential delays in payment or loss of income and capital, underscoring the need for investors to review disclosure documents and consider their own objectives and circumstances before investing.

More about MA Credit Income Trust

MA Credit Income Trust is an Australian private credit investment fund (ARSN 681 002 531) for which Equity Trustees Limited acts as Responsible Entity, with MA Investment Management Pty Ltd and MAAM RE Ltd involved in management and distribution. The fund forms part of the MA Financial group’s private credit offering, earning management and performance fees on direct credit investments and underlying vehicles, and targets income-focused investors in Australia seeking exposure to private credit markets.

data.title: Metrics Real Estate Multi-Strategy Fund Delivers Strong First-Year Returns from Diversified CRE Portfolio
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Metrics Real Estate Multi-Strategy Fund ( (AU:MRE) ).

Metrics Real Estate Multi-Strategy Fund reported a net return of 0.89% for December 2025, contributing to a 24.94% net return and 5.17% distribution yield over the past year since its October 2024 IPO, with units trading at $2.00 against a net asset value of $2.41 and a market capitalisation of $303 million versus NAV of $364 million. The portfolio, constructed via indirect exposure to Metrics’ wholesale real estate debt and equity funds, is heavily weighted to senior-ranking loans with an average loan-to-value ratio of 71%, 151 individual debt positions and 16 equity investments, reflecting a strategy focused on capital preservation, monthly income and equity upside from CRE projects, while positioning the vehicle as a diversified, actively managed listed avenue into private real estate credit and equity markets for investors and platform users.

More about Metrics Real Estate Multi-Strategy Fund

Metrics Real Estate Multi-Strategy Fund (ASX:MRE) is a listed stapled trust that provides investors with exposure to private market commercial real estate across the capital structure, combining a Passive Trust and an Active Trust. Managed by alternative asset manager Metrics Credit Partners, which oversees more than A$30 billion, the fund targets fixed income-style cashflows from senior secured mortgage loans alongside higher-risk, higher-return equity interests in commercial real estate development projects, aiming to deliver diversified access to CRE debt and equity with ASX liquidity.

Average Trading Volume: 245,332

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Learn more about MRE stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: St Barbara’s 15-Mile Hub PFS Confirms High-Return Nova Scotia Gold Project
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from St. Barbara ( (AU:SBM) ).

St Barbara has completed a Pre-Feasibility Study for its 15-Mile Processing Hub Project in Nova Scotia, confirming a capital-efficient, long-life gold operation built around a central processing hub at 15-Mile fed by satellite open pits at Beaver Dam and Cochrane Hill. The study outlines average annual production of about 103,000 ounces over more than 11 years based solely on existing reserves, with forecast life-of-mine all-in sustaining costs of US$1,188 per ounce and initial capital of roughly C$283 million, supported by the reuse of Touquoy processing plant equipment. Project economics are described as outstanding, with strong post-tax NPV and IRR metrics under the gold price assumptions provided, and St Barbara plans to fund development largely from cash flows from the New Simberi Gold Project and the proposed Touquoy restart. The design incorporates substantial reductions in surface disturbance and avoidance of wetlands and watercourses compared with earlier concepts, reflecting feedback from regulators, First Nations and local communities, and the company notes that the work coincides with a more supportive permitting environment in Nova Scotia as it advances toward environmental and social impact assessments and a full feasibility study.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:SBM) stock is a Hold with a A$0.65 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on St. Barbara stock, see the AU:SBM Stock Forecast page.

More about St. Barbara

St Barbara Limited is an Australia-listed gold mining company (ASX: SBM) with operations and development projects in jurisdictions including Canada. In Nova Scotia, Canada, the company is consolidating its most advanced gold projects into a regional hub model, leveraging existing processing infrastructure from the Touquoy plant to support satellite open-pit mines at Beaver Dam and Cochrane Hill, with a strategic focus on capital efficiency and environmentally responsible development.

Average Trading Volume: 8,518,119

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$671.4M

For a thorough assessment of SBM stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Granada Gold Retires $300,000 Debt Through Share Issuance
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Granada Gold Mine Inc ( (TSE:GGM) ).

Granada Gold Mine Inc. has converted $300,000 of debt owed to certain non-arm’s length creditors into equity by issuing 3,000,000 common shares at a deemed price of $0.10 per share, with the new securities subject to a hold period under Canadian securities laws until May 21, 2026. The shares-for-debt transaction helps clean up the company’s balance sheet as it advances exploration and development of its Granada property in Quebec, potentially improving financial flexibility and positioning the company more favourably with investors while drilling activity remains on pause for technical review and market timing.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:GGM) stock is a Hold with a C$0.05 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Granada Gold Mine Inc stock, see the TSE:GGM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TSE:GGM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSE:GGM is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by very weak financial performance (no revenue, ongoing losses, negative cash flow, and negative equity), partially offset by a moderately constructive technical trend with the stock trading above key moving averages. Negative earnings (negative P/E) and no dividend support keep valuation from contributing meaningfully.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:GGM stock, click here.

More about Granada Gold Mine Inc

Granada Gold Mine Inc. is a Canadian mineral exploration and development company focused on its 100%-owned Granada Gold Property near Rouyn-Noranda, Quebec, adjacent to the prolific Cadillac Break. The company controls 14.73 square kilometres of mining leases and claims hosting the historic Granada underground mine and multiple mineralized shear zones, and is in the midst of a major 120,000-metre drilling program, currently paused to allow technical evaluation and to await improved market conditions.

Average Trading Volume: 145,058

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$8.26M

data.title: Beat Holdings Deepens Bitcoin Exposure With Further IBIT Purchases
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Beat Holdings ( (JP:9399) ).

Beat Holdings has expanded its exposure to Bitcoin by making an additional purchase of 12,500 units of the iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) on 21 January 2026, bringing its total holdings to 299,430 units acquired at an average cost of USD55.783 per unit. The group intends to continue accumulating IBIT and will mark these holdings to market each quarter, with management flagging that any material impact on earnings from Bitcoin price movements will be disclosed in a timely manner, underscoring the company’s growing sensitivity to crypto-market volatility and its strategic tilt toward digital-asset-linked investments.

More about Beat Holdings

Beat Holdings Limited, listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Standard Market (code 9399), is a Japan-based group that has been actively allocating capital into digital-assets-related financial products, notably U.S.-listed Bitcoin exchange-traded funds, as part of its investment activities.

Average Trading Volume: 375,776

Current Market Cap: Yen12.2B
